page: Microsoft Azure GS3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the GS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor. This CPU provides 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz, with 40 MB of L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 112.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 229 GB of local storage and contains no GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between compute speed and memory capacity. The server exhibits poor single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and weak multi-core performance. However, its memory subsystem achieves average results for small-block read-only memory bandwidth, though large-block write bandwidth drops to the bottom tier. In application-level benchmarks, the GS3 demonstrates average performance in PassMark database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the GS3 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-frequency CPU performance. Given the average performance in database operations and Redis benchmarks, combined with the 229 GB local storage, this server is configured for relational databases, caching layers, and in-memory data processing. It is less suited for compute-heavy tasks such as large-scale LLM inference or intensive cryptographic operations, where its older CPU architecture and weak single-core performance present bottlenecks.
description: Microsoft Azure GS3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the GS family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 x86_64 processor with 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, supported by 40 MB of L3 cache. The instance features a high memory capacity of 112.0 GB (14.0 GB per core) and includes 229 GB of local storage, with no GPU hardware. Benchmark results indicate weak to poor raw CPU compute performance, but average capabilities in memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis SET operations. This resource profile makes the GS3 qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand high CPU clock speeds. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and in-memory data processing rather than compute-intensive tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 112 GB of RAM, and 229 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and local storage, optimized for database operations and in-memory caching.
